data.selected_code:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg
data.summary: The scrape_season_stats function retrieves per-game NBA player statistics for a specified year from Basketball Reference, filters out repeated headers, adds the year, selects key columns (Player, Year, PTS, AST, TRB, FG%), removes rows with missing key stats, and returns the cleaned DataFrame.

data.instruction: Merge the basic and advanced stats together and return them

data.updated_code:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Advanced stats
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    # Merge basic and advanced stats on Player and Year
    merged_df = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")

    return merged_df
data.instruction: Merge the basic and advanced stats together and return them
data.original_code:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Advanced stats
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    return df_reg

# Model
    for n_est in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_est}...")
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_est,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        # Predict, sort, and evaluate
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
        df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
            df.loc[test_idx]
            .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
            .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
        )

        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_est}: {ndcg:.4f}")

        # Show results
        print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
        plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
        plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

data.summary: The plot_ranking_predictions function visualizes the prediction errors for MVP rankings in a specified year by plotting the difference between predicted and actual ranks for each player, and saves the resulting bar plot as a PNG file.

data.instruction: Change this graph to a grouped bar chart using the coolwarm color pallet

data.updated_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year as a grouped bar chart.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Create grouped bar chart: True Rank vs Predicted Rank for each player
    x = np.arange(len(year_df))
    width = 0.35

    plt.bar(x - width/2, year_df["Rank"], width, label="True Rank", color=sns.color_palette("coolwarm", n_colors=2)[0])
    plt.bar(x + width/2, year_df["PredictedRank"], width, label="Predicted Rank", color=sns.color_palette("coolwarm", n_colors=2)[1])

    plt.xticks(x, year_df["Name"], rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year}) - Grouped Bar Chart")
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
data.instruction: Change this graph to a grouped bar chart using the coolwarm color pallet

data.selected_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
data.summary: The plot_ranking_predictions function generates and saves a bar plot visualizing the prediction errors (difference between predicted and true ranks) for each player in a specified year, highlighting how well the ranking model performed for that year's MVP predictions.

data.updated_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    # Grouped bar chart: show both True Rank and Predicted Rank side by side for each player
    bar_width = 0.35
    indices = np.arange(len(year_df))
    plt.bar(indices - bar_width/2, year_df["Rank"], bar_width, label="True Rank")
    plt.bar(indices + bar_width/2, year_df["PredictedRank"], bar_width, label="Predicted Rank")
    plt.xticks(indices, year_df["Name"], rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.
